versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 19 Therefore this is what the LORD says:
“If you return, I will restore you;
you will stand in My presence.
And if you speak words that are noble instead of worthless,
It is they who must turn to you,
but you must not turn to them.
20 Then I will make you a wall to this people,
for I am with you to save and deliver you,
21 I will deliver you from the hand of the wicked
and redeem you from the grasp of the ruthless.”

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 15**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The LORD’s Promise** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 15** remains the first control on meaning.
